books.title: The Student Bible Atlas, Revised Edition
books.author: Tim Dowley
books.rationale: This is a simple, inexpensive Bible atlas that provides full-page maps of the biblical lands of the Old and New Testaments. In Bible lessons, students will be directed to look at specific pages. This resource is for Grades 3-8 ONLY.
books.sharedText:
Old Testament: Grades 3-6
New Testament: Grades 3-6
New Testament: Grades 7-8
Old Testament: Grades 7-12
Story of Arithmetic: Part 1
Story of Geometry: Part 1

books.title: ESV Large Print Bible
books.rationale: This large-print ESV Bible uses 12-point type for ease of reading
books.sharedText:
Old Testament: Grade 2
New Testament: Grade 2
Old Testament: Grades 3-6
New Testament: Grades 3-6

books.title: The New Manners and Customs of Bible Times
books.author: Ralph Gower
books.rationale: Through illustrations, photos, and clear explanations, this best-selling reference book shows what life was like in Bible times and makes Scripture come to life as students imagine living in that time and place. We will assign certain sections before or after Bible readings that will help students understand the context of the passage and gain more insight into the culture and geography of Israel.
books.sharedText:
New Testament: Grades 3-6
Old Testament: Grades 3-6

books.title: Hudson Taylor: Deep in the Heart of China
books.author: Janet & Geoff Benge
books.rationale: Hudson Taylor (1832-1905), the founder of China Inland Mission, continues to inspire through the story of his love for the people of China and his trust in the Lord to provide financially for his ministry. Taylor faced persecution and loss in his own life in order to bring the gospel message to the “ripe harvest fields in China.”

books.title: Trial by Poison: Mary Slessor
books.author: Dave & Neta Jackson
books.rationale: Born in Scotland, Mary Slessor (1848-1915) became a missionary to Calabar, Africa (now Southern Nigeria) at the age of 27. As she moved further inland, she brought the gospel to unreached people and rescued many twin babies, who otherwise would have been killed because of the mistaken beliefs of their parents.
